title: Viking Therapeutics' Early Clinical Data Continue to Build Case for Its Oral Obesity Drug
text: Viking Therapeutics' oral obesity drug VK2735 continues to generate positive clinical data that demonstrates its potential to compete in a crowded obesity market. The company's Phase 2 VENTURE-Oral Dosing trial achieved its primary and secondary endpoints, demonstrating statistically significant weight reduction with up to 12.2% (26.6 lbs) mean weight loss at the highest dose compared to 1.3% for placebo. The company's oral formulation showed dose-dependent weight loss in Phase 1 testing, with the highest dose (100 mg daily) achieving 8.2% weight reduction from baseline over 28 days. With progressive weight loss curves suggesting potential for further improvement with longer dosing periods, investors are watching to see if VK2735 can differentiate itself from existing obesity drugs. The injectable version of VK2735 achieved up to 14.7% weight loss over 13 weeks in Phase 2 testing, outperforming some competitors' drugs at similar timepoints. Viking is advancing VK2735 into Phase 3 development with both injectable and oral formulations, positioning the company for potential market leadership in the growing obesity therapeutics space. The company ended 2024 with $903 million in cash, providing ample runway for clinical development.
